shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: Cap near console shifter base.
Pry off the cap or cover if present using your ignition key or a small flathead.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
Hellcat / Redeye / Demon: parking pawl is extremely strong — hold slot firmly for 3–5 seconds before attempting to move the selector.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood, driver side.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
If power cannot be restored, use the shift lock slot above — Cap near console shifter base.
vehicles.notes: Manual: clutch to N. RWD. High-perf models have strong parking pawl — apply firm pressure on slot.
vehicles.towMethod: Lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air or flatbed. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: RWD: Lift from REAR. Rear (drive) wheels are lifted in the air. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION OPTION: If equipped with manual trans, no shift lock — depress clutch and select Neutral. No override slot needed.

knownIssues.title: Strong Parking Pawl on High-Performance Models
knownIssues.detail: Hellcat, Redeye, and Demon models have an exceptionally strong parking pawl that requires significant force on the manual override slot. Press firmly and hold for 3-5 seconds before attempting to move the shifter. Operators sometimes give up too early thinking the slot isn't working.

battery.warnings: Hellcat/Redeye/Demon: parking pawl is very strong — press slot firmly for 3-5 seconds.

shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: Cap near console shifter base.
Pry off the cap or cover if present using your ignition key or a small flathead.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood, driver side.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
If power cannot be restored, use the shift lock slot above — Cap near console shifter base.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: RWD: Lift from REAR. Rear (drive) wheels are lifted in the air. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
AWD VARIANT: Use flatbed. If no flatbed, wheel-lift with dollies on free-rolling axle.

vehicles.slot: Electronic selector — slot near selector base
shift: Turn the Dodge Charger Daytona EV to ON/Ready mode.
Shift lock slot: Electronic selector — slot near selector base.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the slot and press firmly.
Press the brake pedal while holding the slot.
Move selector to N.
FLATBED ONLY — do not tow with driven wheels on the ground.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood (12V auxiliary). 12V AUXILIARY BATTERY ONLY — never touch the high-voltage battery pack.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to N normally.
FLATBED ONLY.
towNotes: ELECTRIC VEHICLE: FLATBED ONLY — never tow with any driven wheels on the ground. Regenerative braking system can damage motor/inverter.
If vehicle must be moved short distance without flatbed, use dollies under ALL driven wheels.
12V battery ONLY for jumping — NEVER touch or access the high-voltage (HV) system. HV components are marked with ORANGE cables/covers.

battery.warnings: NEVER touch HV battery. Flatbed only.

vehicles.slot: Cap at console shifter (older) or slot near electronic selector (newer)
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the electronic selector override: Cap at console shifter (older) or slot near electronic selector (newer).
Remove trim panel or cover if present — use a trim pry tool or your fingernail at the edge.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the exposed slot.
Press firmly inward and hold.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Press the N button or move the electronic selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and select N normally on the electronic selector.
Electronic selectors require power — mechanical override is a last resort only.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. AWD/4WD: transfer case to 2WD or flatbed. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.

tc.type: Electric push-button (NV244 or NV140 depending on year)
positions: 2WD — rear wheels only — REQUIRED for road towing
4WD Auto — automatic AWD — NOT safe for towing
4WD Hi — part-time 4WD — NOT safe for dry-road towing
4WD Lo — low range — NEVER tow in this position
steps: Press the 2WD button on the transfer case control.
Safe to lift front (drive) wheels once confirmed in 2WD.
IF BATTERY IS DEAD: Jump battery first to power electronic TC, shift to 2WD, then disconnect. If unable to shift, FLATBED ONLY.

towNotes: ELECTRONIC TRANSFER CASE: Requires 12V power to shift. If battery is dead, jump the battery FIRST, then shift TC to 2WD before disconnecting.
DEAD BATTERY FALLBACK: If TC cannot be shifted to 2WD, USE FLATBED. Do NOT tow with TC in 4WD/4H/4L — will destroy transfer case and driveline.

Locate the shift lock release: Cap near console shifter base.
Pry off the cap or cover if present using your ignition key or a small flathead.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.

vehicles.towMethod: Lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air, or flatbed. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.

shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: Cap near console shifter base.
Pry off the cap or cover if present using your ignition key or a small flathead.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
If power cannot be restored, use the shift lock slot above — Cap near console shifter base.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air. AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.

vehicles.slot: Cap at side of console shifter base
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: Cap at side of console shifter base.
Pry off the cap or cover if present using your ignition key or a small flathead.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
If power cannot be restored, use the shift lock slot above — Cap at side of console shifter base.
vehicles.notes: 4WD: transfer case. Manual: clutch to N.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD/2WD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. 4WD: transfer case to 2H first. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.

tc.type: Manual floor lever or electric push-button depending on trim
positions: 2H — rear wheels only — REQUIRED for all road towing
4L — low range — NEVER tow in this position
steps: MANUAL: Stop completely. Shift TC floor lever to 2H.
ELECTRIC: Slow under 3 MPH. Press 2H. Confirm indicator.
Safe to lift rear wheels in 2H.
NEVER tow in 4H on dry pavement or in 4L.
IF BATTERY IS DEAD: Jump battery first to power electronic TC, shift to 2WD, then disconnect. If unable to shift, FLATBED ONLY.

towNotes: MANUAL TRANSFER CASE (floor lever): Vehicle must be STOPPED to shift. If lever is stiff, rock vehicle slightly in neutral while applying firm pressure.
ELECTRONIC TRANSFER CASE: Requires 12V power to shift. If battery is dead, jump the battery FIRST, then shift TC to 2WD before disconnecting.
DEAD BATTERY FALLBACK: If TC cannot be shifted to 2WD, USE FLATBED. Do NOT tow with TC in 4WD/4H/4L — will destroy transfer case and driveline.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION OPTION: If equipped with manual trans, no shift lock — depress clutch and select Neutral. No override slot needed.
4WD DOLLY FALLBACK: If transfer case CANNOT be shifted to 2WD (dead battery, mechanical failure), either use flatbed OR wheel-lift with dollies on the opposite axle.

vehicles.slot: Manual: no shift lock. Auto: slot near selector
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: Manual: no shift lock. Auto: slot near selector.
Pry off the cap or cover if present using your ignition key or a small flathead.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood, passenger side near firewall.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
If power cannot be restored, use the shift lock slot above — Manual: no shift lock. Auto: slot near selector.
vehicles.notes: Most Vipers are manual. SRT/ACR: manual only.
vehicles.warn: EXTREMELY LOW CLEARANCE — flatbed ONLY. Side lift recommended.
vehicles.towMethod: FLATBED ONLY — extremely low clearance, side lift recommended
towNotes: RWD: Lift from REAR. Rear (drive) wheels are lifted in the air. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
LOW CLEARANCE VEHICLE: Use flatbed. Check ground clearance before attempting any wheel-lift — front splitter/rear diffuser damage is common.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION OPTION: If equipped with manual trans, no shift lock — depress clutch and select Neutral. No override slot needed.

knownIssues.title: Extremely Low Ground Clearance — Side Lift Required
knownIssues.detail: The Viper has approximately 3.7 inches of ground clearance. Standard flatbed ramps cannot be used without damaging the front splitter and underbody. Side-lift equipment or very shallow approach ramps with extensions are required. Never winch from the front.

knownIssues.title: Most Vipers Are Manual Transmission
knownIssues.detail: The vast majority of Vipers have manual transmissions — no shift lock, no neutral override needed. Simply depress the clutch and select neutral. Automatic Viper variants are extremely rare. Verify before spending time on neutral override procedures.

battery.location: Under hood, passenger side near firewall
battery.warnings: Side-lift or special ramps only. Carbon ceramic brakes on some trims — no metal chocks against rotors.

vehicles.slot: Small cap near selector base on center console
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
AUTOMATIC: Locate shift lock slot — small cap near selector base on center console.
Pry off cap. Insert key and press firmly.
While holding, press brake. Move selector to Neutral.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION: depress clutch fully and move gear lever to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — under the hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front.

vehicles.slot: Small cap near selector base on center console
shift: Turn ignition to ON (key to Run position — do not start).
Locate the shift lock override: small cap near selector base on center console.
Pry off the plastic cap with your ignition key or a small flathead screwdriver.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — under the hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front.

vehicles.slot: Column shifter — slot on STEERING COLUMN. Console shift: cap near selector base.
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
COLUMN SHIFT (most trims): slot is on the STEERING COLUMN — insert key into column slot, press firmly, press brake, move column lever to Neutral.
CONSOLE SHIFT: cap near selector base on console. Pry off. Insert key, press, press brake, move to Neutral.
MANUAL: depress clutch, move lever to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — under the hood, driver side.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
4WD: transfer case must be in 2WD before towing.
vehicles.notes: Many trims have column shifter — slot is on steering column, not console.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD/2WD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. 4WD: TC to 2H, then lift from rear.
towNotes: Column shifter on many trims — slot on steering column.
RWD: lift from rear. 4WD: TC to 2H.
